title: Stellenbosh
content:
آ آ آ آ The general ï؟½eard the firinï؟½ on the flank,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ ï؟½e sent a mounted man to bring ï؟½im back
آ آ آ آ The silly, pushinï؟½ personï؟½s name anï؟½ rank
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Ooï؟½d dared to answer Brother Boerï؟½s attack:
آ آ آ آ For there might ï؟½ave been a serious engagement,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ ï؟½e might ï؟½ave wasted ï؟½alf a dozen men;
آ آ آ آ So ï؟½e ordered ï؟½im to stop ï؟½is operations round the kopjes,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ ï؟½e told ï؟½im off before the Staff at ten!
آ آ آ آ And it all goes into the laundry,
آ آ آ آ But it never comes out in the wash,
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Ow weï؟½re sugared about by the old men
آ آ آ آ (ï؟½Eavy sterned amateur old men!)
آ آ آ آ That ï؟½amper anï؟½ ï؟½inder anï؟½ scold men
آ آ آ آ For fear oï؟½ Stellenbosh!*

آ آ آ آ The General ï؟½ad ï؟½produced a great effect,ï؟½
آ آ آ آ The General ï؟½ad the country cleared, almost;
آ آ آ آ The General ï؟½ï؟½ad no reason to expect,ï؟½
آ آ آ آ And the Boers ï؟½ad us bloominï؟½ well on toast!
آ آ آ آ For we might ï؟½ave crossed the drift before the twilight,
آ آ آ آ Instead oï؟½ sitting down anï؟½ takinï؟½ root;
آ آ آ آ But we was not allowed, so the Boojers scooped the crowd,
آ آ آ آ To the last survivinï؟½ bandolier anï؟½ boot.

آ آ آ آ The General saw the farmï؟½ouse in ï؟½is rear,
آ آ آ آ With its stoep so nicely shaded from the sun;
آ آ آ آ Sez ï؟½e, ï؟½Iï؟½ll pitch my tabernacle ï؟½ere,ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ ï؟½e kept us muckinï؟½ round till ï؟½e ï؟½ad done.
آ آ آ آ For ï؟½e might ï؟½ave caught the confluent pneumonia
آ آ آ آ From sleepinï؟½ in his gaiters in the dew;
آ آ آ آ So ï؟½e took a book anï؟½ dozed while the other columns closed
آ آ آ آ And De Wetï؟½s commando out anï؟½ trickled through!

آ آ آ آ The General saw the mountain-range ahead,
آ آ آ آ With their ï؟½elios showinï؟½ saucy on the ï؟½eight,
آ آ آ آ So ï؟½e ï؟½eld us to the level ground instead,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ telegraphed the Boojers would nï؟½t fight.
آ آ آ آ For ï؟½e might ï؟½ave gone anï؟½ sprayed ï؟½em with a pompom,
آ آ آ آ Or ï؟½e might ï؟½ave slung a squadron out to see,
آ آ آ آ Butï؟½e was nï؟½t takinï؟½ chances in them ï؟½igh anï؟½ ï؟½ostile kranzes,
آ آ آ آ He was markinï؟½ time to earn a K.C.B

آ آ آ آ The General got ï؟½is decorations thick
آ آ آ آ (The men that backed ï؟½is lies could not complain),
آ آ آ آ The Staff ï؟½ad D.S.O.ï؟½s till we was sick,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ the soldier, ï؟½ad the work to do again!
آ آ آ آ For ï؟½e might ï؟½ave known the District was an ï؟½otbed,
آ آ آ آ Instead of ï؟½andinï؟½ over, upside-down,
آ آ آ آ To a man ï؟½oo ï؟½ad to fight ï؟½alf a year to put it right,
آ آ آ آ While the General went anï؟½ slandered ï؟½im in town!

آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ it all went into the laundry,
آ آ آ آ But it never came out in the wash.
آ آ آ آ We were sugared about by the old men
آ آ آ آ (Panicky, perishinï؟½ old men)
آ آ آ آ That ï؟½amper anï؟½ ï؟½inder anï؟½ scold men
آ آ آ آ For fear oï؟½ Stellenbosh!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Rudyard Kipling represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Rudyard Kipling's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
